report_name: Ex-FBI Official Urges Terror Designations for Ransomware Gangs Attacking Hospitals

threat_actors.name: Ransomware Groups Targeting Hospitals
threat_actors.description: A general class of cybercriminal groups that conduct ransomware attacks against hospitals and other critical infrastructure. The article discusses a proposal to designate these groups as terrorist organizations if their actions knowingly endanger human life.

identities.name: Cynthia Kaiser
identities.description: Former FBI Cyber Division Deputy Assistant Director who proposed designating ransomware groups that attack hospitals as terrorist organizations.

identities.name: Federal Bureau of Investigation
identities.description: The U.S. federal law enforcement agency where Cynthia Kaiser formerly served as a Cyber Division Deputy Assistant Director.

identities.name: House Homeland Security Committee
identities.description: The U.S. congressional committee that heard testimony from Cynthia Kaiser regarding the proposal to treat certain ransomware attacks as terrorism.

identities.name: U.S. Department of the Treasury
identities.description: The U.S. government agency that would be empowered to block assets of designated ransomware groups under Executive Order 13224, as per the proposal.

identities.name: U.S. Department of Justice
identities.description: The U.S. government agency that would be responsible for prosecuting attackers, potentially with homicide charges under the federal felony murder rule, if the proposal is adopted.

identities.name: U.S. Department of State
identities.description: A U.S. government agency that would be involved in the analysis and designation of ransomware groups as terrorist organizations.

identities.name: Healthcare Sector
identities.description: The critical infrastructure sector, specifically hospitals, identified as the primary victim class in the proposal to designate ransomware attacks as terrorism.
